title: Malaysia's Anwar to Push ASEAN-China Fast-Track of South China Sea Code of Conduct at May Summit
detail: Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim announced on April 7, 2026, that he would urge ASEAN member states and China to fast-track negotiations on a binding Code of Conduct (COC) for the South China Sea at the upcoming ASEAN Leaders' Summit in May 2026. Malaysia holds the ASEAN chairmanship for 2026 and has targeted completion of the COC framework by July 2026 — a deadline experts have generally called 'simply not achievable.' ASEAN Secretary-General Kao Kim Hourn and Malaysia's ASEAN presidency have described 2026 as a 'make or break' year for the COC. Key sticking points remain: the geographic scope of the COC (whether it covers the Paracels and Scarborough Shoal in addition to the Spratlys), whether it will be legally binding, and whether it will prohibit third-party military activities in the South China Sea — the last point a priority for China but opposed by the United States and the Philippines. China's Wu Shicun, founding president of China's National Institute for South China Sea Studies, assessed the probability of a 2026 COC signing as '100 per cent not likely.' The Philippines' ASEAN engagement focused on protecting intra-ASEAN cohesion ahead of May.

title: AFP-US Forces Break Ground on Evacuation-Ready Facility in Davao del Norte as Balikatan 2026 Prep Intensifies
detail: Armed Forces of the Philippines and US military personnel broke ground on April 7, 2026, on a new evacuation-ready multi-purpose facility in Barangay Tibulao, Carmen, Davao del Norte — one of the pre-Balikatan construction activities in advance of the exercise starting approximately April 20. The facility is designed for noncombatant evacuation operations (NEO) and disaster response scenarios that are part of Balikatan 2026's expanded exercise portfolio. The groundbreaking was among the most visible of 500+ bilateral military activities approved under 2026 alliance planning guidance — the highest count in US-Philippines alliance history. Balikatan 2026, billed as the largest in history with approximately 16,000+ US, Philippine, Japanese, Australian, Canadian, and French troops, will also include cyber defense, space operations, and live-fire exercises. The exercise is expected to feature NMESIS anti-ship missile deployments at Batanes Islands, providing coverage of the Luzon Strait between Luzon and Taiwan.
